factors.name: Dow earnings & guidance
factors.summary: SoftBank surging 11%+ on AI optimism, Lenovo +13% on AI earnings; Boeing eying delivery rebound. Mixed signal—AI tailwinds vs. aerospace/industrial headwinds (Boeing delays, SpaceX scrubs).

factors.name: Fed policy & rate expectations
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair Friday (hawkish lean vs Blankfein era). Rising talk of hawkish Fed tone per FXStreet; USD at 6-week high (DXY 99.25). Rate-sensitive Dow names (utilities, dividend payers) under pressure.

factors.name: US-Iran ceasefire progress & oil
factors.summary: Oil down ~$1 on peace talk optimism (WTI $96.80, Brent mid-$80s expected per Bloomberg survey). Eases energy shock inflation fears and reduces geopolitical risk premium. Supports consumer & transport costs.

factors.name: Japan CPI & BOJ
factors.summary: Japan core CPI 1.4% YoY (4-year low), well below 1.7% forecast, but ING still expects June BOJ hike. Soft inflation may delay BoJ tightening vs. near-term expectations; mixed for yen and global growth.

factors.name: China data & FDI policy
factors.summary: NDRC softens tone on foreign tech investment (no outright ban), but China's National Team cutting ETF stakes 90% in H1 2026—mixed liquidity signal. Caterpillar & materials demand outlook unclear.

factors.name: Dollar strength
factors.summary: USD at 6-week high (DXY ~99.25) pressures multinational earnings translation; Dow has ~50% international revenue. EUR/USD weak at 1.1610, USD/JPY at 159.10; dollar carry-trade tightening.

factors.name: US labor & growth momentum
factors.summary: US resilient per 'robust labour data' note; Australia jobless rate up (RBA hike bets fade). Mixed global growth backdrop—no fresh US PMI/ISM data in this window.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions
factors.summary: Trump sends 5,000 more troops to Poland (week after cancelling 4,000); Iran nuclear deal fragile; Alberta referendum on separation looms. Elevated tail risks & policy uncertainty.

factors.name: Valuation & market structure
factors.summary: MarketWatch warns 'Buffett Indicator' signals market top (230% valuation gap); technical sell signals flashing despite Nvidia optimism. Fragility narrative building despite upside narrative.

factors.name: Price action momentum
factors.summary: US30 +0.23% in 4h, +0.53% in 24h—modest rally underway but not yet extended. Move is in-progress (Phase 2), requires retest to confirm strength.

screening.narrative: The Dow is in a modest Phase 2 rally (+0.53% in 24h) on US-Iran ceasefire optimism and select mega-cap AI wins (SoftBank +11%, Lenovo +13%), offsetting hawkish Fed chair Warsh signals and persistent dollar strength (DXY 99.25). Japan's unexpectedly soft CPI (1.4% vs 1.7% expected) eases near-term BoJ tightening fears but also signals global growth slowness. However, valuation warnings ('Buffett Indicator' signals excess) and elevated geopolitical risk (troops to Poland, Iran deal fragility, Alberta referendum) create a conflict between bullish tactical catalysts and bearish structural headwinds.
catalysts_ahead: US Michigan Consumer Sentiment (May; due Friday AM before market close at 2 PM ET for bonds)
Memorial Day (markets closed Monday 5/25; shortened Friday; full reopening Tuesday 5/26)
RBNZ rate decision (May 27—expected hold at 2.25%, but Q3 hike expectations rising)
Dow component earnings flow (BA, CAT, GE, JPM, V guidance in coming weeks)
Fed Warsh inaugural commentary/signals (new chair tone-setting)
China macro (any PMI/growth data releases; NDRC/PBOC policy signals)
Oil/energy market developments (Iran deal closure vs. stall-out could swing +/- $10/barrel)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran talks collapse or geopolitical escalation spikes, oil could spike >$110/bbl, triggering inflation fears and Flight-to-Quality (bonds rally, equities dump). Conversely, if Fed Warsh cuts rates faster than expected or US-Iran deal finalizes, Dow rallies hard through 50,500. Valuation risk (Buffett Indicator warning) means upside is capped if growth disappoints or tech earnings disappoint. China's ETF liquidation may trigger unexpected liquidity drain.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway); wait for pullback retest of 50,150–50,200 support to confirm strength before buying. On break above 50,500, reassess—extended moves often fizzle. Avoid chasing here; risk/reward favors patience into Memorial Day holiday gap and next major catalyst (BoJ/PMI/earnings).
